ogTitle: Fern Gully vs Fernances Crossing: Climate Safety

heading: Fern Gully vs Fernances Crossing — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fern Gully, New South Wales and Fernances Crossing, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fernances Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Fern Gully (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fern Gully or Fernances Crossing safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fern Gully has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Fernances Crossing scores 20/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fern Gully scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Fernances Crossing scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fern Gully or Fernances Crossing?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fernances Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
